name: McKinley Assassinated, Theodore Roosevelt Becomes U.S President
description: McKinley had overseen the brutal suppression of the Philippine independence movement, killing hundreds of thousands. Roosevelt continued this imperial project, treating colonised peoples as subjects to be managed for American commercial interests.

name: Cuba Gains Independence from U.S
description: Cuba gained nominal independence but the Platt Amendment granted the U.S. the right to intervene militarily at will, making Cuba an effective American neocolony. The Cuban people would wait until 1959 for genuine liberation.

name: Taft Becomes U.S President
description: Taft escalated dollar diplomacy, deploying U.S. troops across Central America and the Caribbean to force open markets and protect American corporations. Mexico, Nicaragua, and Honduras were subjected to military intervention to serve Wall Street interests.

name: End of Chinese Empire
description: The collapse of the Qing Dynasty came after a century of imperialist humiliation by Western powers who had forced opium markets, extracted enormous indemnities, and carved China into spheres of foreign influence.

name: Sinking of Titanic
description: The Titanic disaster was a monument to class inequality. Third-class passengers were reportedly locked below decks, and survival rates tracked perfectly with wealth. The rich escaped; the workers drowned. Capitalism made even death unequal.

name: Wilson Becomes U.S President
description: Wilson's rhetoric of self-determination applied only to white Europeans. His presidency saw U.S. military occupations of Haiti, the Dominican Republic, Nicaragua, and Mexico, crushing socialist movements and protecting American financial interests.

name: World War 1 Begins
description: An imperialist war for colonial markets and resources dragged the working class of Europe into industrial slaughter. Workers killed workers on behalf of capitalists and kings while arms manufacturers and bankers profited enormously from the carnage.

name: Easter Rising in Ireland
description: Irish workers and republicans heroically rose against centuries of British colonial occupation. James Connolly led the socialist Irish Citizen Army; Britain executed the leaders, but their sacrifice galvanised the independence movement.

name: Russian Revolution Ends the Russian Empire, Begins Russian Civil War
description: Workers, soldiers, and peasants overthrew the Tsar and the bourgeois Provisional Government. Western powers immediately began arming counter-revolutionary White Army forces, desperate to strangle the world's first workers' state in its cradle.

name: October Bolshevik Revolution
description: The Bolsheviks led the working class to seize power and end Russia's involvement in an imperialist war that had already killed millions. The revolution terrified the capitalist world, which launched 14 national invasions to reverse it.

name: Balfour Declaration
description: Britain promised Palestinian land it did not own to the Zionist movement, in exchange for Zionist lobbying to bring America into WWI. The Declaration served British imperial interests and planted the seeds of a century of Palestinian dispossession.

name: World War 1 Ends
description: The imperialist war ended with ten million workers dead. At Versailles, the colonial powers carved up the world among themselves, ignoring the demands of colonised peoples for self-determination. The punitive terms imposed on Germany would breed fascism.

name: Irish War of Independence Begins
description: The Irish people launched a guerrilla war against centuries of British colonial occupation, refusing to accept a fraudulent Home Rule settlement. Britain responded with the Black and Tans, a paramilitary force that terrorised the civilian population.

name: League of Nations Formed
description: A club of imperial powers designed to legitimise the colonial world order, not to challenge it. Colonial peoples were deemed not ready for self-determination. The League enforced the post-WWI settlement that protected Western imperial interests.

name: Harding Becomes U.S President
description: The Harding administration was defined by the Teapot Dome scandal, in which oil companies bribed cabinet officials for drilling rights. It was a textbook demonstration of how American capitalism uses political power to enrich its ruling class.

name: Irish War of Independence Ends
description: The Anglo-Irish Treaty partitioned Ireland, leaving six counties under British rule. Pro-Treaty forces accepted the settlement under threat of renewed all-out war, effectively doing the British Empire's work in dividing the Irish republican movement.

name: British Mandate for Palestine
description: The League of Nations rubber-stamped Britain's control of Palestine, allowing it to play Arab and Jewish communities against each other to maintain imperial dominance. The Mandate systematically facilitated the dispossession of the Palestinian majority.

name: Irish Civil War Begins
description: Anti-Treaty republicans who refused to accept partition and British dominion status fought the pro-Treaty government. Churchill explicitly threatened devastation if the Treaty was rejected; Britain had deliberately put Irish people in the position of fighting each other.

name: Irish Freestate & N.I Established
description: The Irish Free State emerged as a conservative, Catholic, capitalist state that preserved British economic interests and landlord power. Partition left a sectarian statelet in the north where Catholics faced systematic discrimination for generations.

name: End of Ottoman Empire
description: Western powers dismembered the Ottoman Empire through the secret Sykes-Picot Agreement, drawing borders to serve their oil and strategic interests with no regard for the peoples who lived there, creating conflicts that persist to this day.

name: March on Rome Brings Mussolini into Power
description: Italian industrialists and landowners bankrolled Mussolini's Blackshirts specifically to crush the workers' movement and communist party. Fascism was capitalism's violent response to the threat of revolution - the open terrorist dictatorship of the bourgeoisie.

name: USSR Established
description: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics was born, offering for the first time in history an alternative to capitalist exploitation for working people everywhere. Despite Western military invasion and economic blockade, socialism in one country had survived.

name: Irish Civil War Ends
description: Pro-Treaty forces defeated the republicans, but the bitterness of the conflict - which Britain had deliberately engineered - shaped Irish politics for generations, keeping Irish workers divided along civil war lines rather than class lines.

name: Coolidge Becomes U.S President
description: Coolidge proclaimed that the business of America is business, slashing taxes for the wealthy and dismantling worker protections. The speculative boom of the Roaring Twenties enriched the few and was built on a foundation that would catastrophically collapse.

name: Death of Vladimir Lenin
description: Lenin, who led the world's first socialist revolution, defeated Western military invasion, and wrote brilliantly on imperialism as the highest stage of capitalism, died at 53. His death opened a power struggle in the Soviet state he had built from nothing.

name: Pahlavi Dynasty Begins in Iran
description: Reza Khan's monarchy was backed by Britain to suppress the socialist and communist movements that threatened British oil interests in Iran. The Pahlavi dynasty would prove a faithful servant of Western imperialism until 1979.

name: Chinese Civil War Begins
description: Chiang Kai-shek, financed by Shanghai bankers and backed by Western powers, massacred thousands of Communist workers in the Shanghai Coup. Western imperialism was desperate to prevent a socialist revolution in the world's most populous country.

name: Wall Street Crash, Great Depression Begins
description: Speculative capitalism collapsed, throwing tens of millions of workers worldwide into unemployment and poverty. The Great Depression proved the fundamental instability of the capitalist system and drove millions toward socialist and communist parties.

name: Chinese Soviet Republic Established
description: Chinese workers and peasants established their own revolutionary government in Jiangxi province, fighting against the Nationalist regime backed by American loans, British advisers, and German military officers.

name: Hitler Comes into Power
description: German industrialists and financiers backed Hitler specifically as a weapon against the Communist Party and trade unions. Western powers spent years appeasing him, many hoping he would turn his armies east against the Soviet Union.

name: F.D.R Becomes U.S President
description: Roosevelt's New Deal was not socialism but a capitalist rescue operation, designed to stabilise a collapsing system against the threat of revolution. It saved American capitalism from itself while leaving the fundamental structures of exploitation intact.

name: The Long March
description: The Red Army's heroic 6,000-mile retreat through impossible terrain, pursued by Nationalist forces backed by Western material support, forged the revolutionary will and leadership that would ultimately liberate China from feudalism and imperialism.

name: Spanish Civil War Begins
description: Fascists backed by Hitler's Germany and Mussolini's Italy attacked Spain's elected Republic. Britain and France hid behind non-intervention, secretly relieved that Franco was crushing the Spanish left. Only the Soviet Union provided meaningful aid to the Republic.

name: Great Palestinian Revolt Begins
description: Palestinians rose heroically against British colonialism and the organised dispossession of their land. Britain responded with mass executions, collective punishment, and house demolitions - the same techniques Israel later inherited and continues to use.

name: Second Sino-Japanese War Begins
description: Japan's imperialist expansion was tacitly enabled by Western powers who had long accepted Japanese aggression when directed at China. Only after Pearl Harbor threatened their own interests would they take effective action against Japanese militarism.

name: The Nanjing Massacre
description: Japanese imperialist forces murdered up to 300,000 Chinese civilians in one of the worst atrocities of the 20th century. Western governments largely looked the other way, having spent years accommodating Japanese imperialism in Asia.

name: Second United Front, Pause in Chinese Civil War
description: The Communist Party put the national interest above its own, suspending the civil war to form a united front against Japanese imperialism - demonstrating the revolutionary patriotism of a party that put the people's welfare first.

name: Spanish Civil War Ends, Franco Comes into Power
description: With Western democracies refusing arms to the Republic, Franco's fascists triumphed. Only the Soviet Union had provided meaningful support to the Spanish people. The West's complicity in fascism's victory was a betrayal of working people everywhere.

name: World War 2 Begins
description: Western appeasement of Hitler at Munich had emboldened the Nazi war machine. Many in the British and French ruling class had hoped to direct German aggression eastward to destroy the Soviet Union. Their cynical calculation cost the world 80 million lives.

name: Great Palestinian Revolt Ends
description: Britain violently crushed the Palestinian uprising, executing its leaders and disarming Arab communities. This deliberate disarmament left Palestinians defenceless against the Zionist militias that would ethnically cleanse them a decade later.

name: Churchill Becomes Prime Minister
description: Churchill, architect of the Bengal famine and lifelong defender of the British Empire, became Prime Minister. He would later say he had not become His Majesty's First Minister in order to preside over the liquidation of the British Empire.

name: Nazi Invasion of USSR
description: Hitler launched his genocidal assault on the USSR, killing 27 million Soviet people. The Red Army bore the overwhelming burden of defeating Nazi Germany while Western powers delayed the second front for years, hoping the two systems would bleed each other dry.

name: Pearl Harbor
description: America's economic warfare against Japan - oil embargoes and asset freezes - provoked the Pearl Harbor attack. The U.S. had been supplying both sides of the Japan-China war for years, profiting from the conflict before being forced to enter it.

name: Manhattan Project Begins
description: The U.S. secretly developed atomic weapons. As later revealed, the bombs were dropped on Japan not primarily to end the war - which was already effectively over - but to intimidate the Soviet Union and establish American nuclear hegemony in the post-war world.

name: Bengal Famine
description: Churchill's deliberate policies - exporting food from India while refusing emergency imports - caused a famine that killed between 2 and 3 million Bengalis. Churchill reportedly asked why Gandhi had not yet died, expressing frustration the famine had not killed the independence leader.

name: Truman Becomes U.S President
description: Truman immediately sabotaged FDR's cooperative relationship with the Soviet Union, launching the Cold War. He ordered atomic bombs dropped on two Japanese cities filled with civilian workers, not for military necessity but to demonstrate American power to the USSR.

name: Execution of Benito Mussolini
description: Italian Communist partisans delivered justice to the fascist dictator. Mussolini had been backed by Western business interests for two decades, celebrated by Western newspapers as a bulwark against communism until he became inconvenient.

name: Death of Adolf Hitler
description: The Soviet Red Army, at the cost of 27 million lives, shattered the Nazi war machine that Western capital had enabled and that Western governments had spent years appeasing. The USSR's sacrifice in defeating fascism is the defining military achievement of the 20th century.

name: Atomic Bombings of Japan, World War 2 Ends
description: The U.S. dropped atomic bombs on the civilian populations of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, killing over 200,000 people. Historians increasingly recognise this as the opening shot of the Cold War - a demonstration of American power directed as much at the Soviet Union as at Japan.

name: UN Established
description: A new world order was established with the Western imperial powers holding permanent veto rights. The UN was designed to legitimise American hegemony while providing just enough multilateral cover to manage the global system that served Western capitalist interests.

name: Chinese Civil War Resumes
description: The U.S. massively armed and funded the corrupt Nationalist regime with billions of dollars in weapons and loans in a desperate bid to prevent the Communist revolution. Despite this, the Chinese people's movement proved unstoppable.

name: UN Partition Plan for Palestine
description: Western powers pushed through the partition of Palestine over the objections of its Arab majority population. The plan allocated 56% of Palestine to a Jewish minority that owned 7% of the land - a colonial dispossession laundered through international legality.

name: Cold War Begins, CIA Created
description: The CIA was created as a global instrument for crushing socialist and communist movements, funding right-wing parties, backing military coups, and assassinating left-wing leaders across the world. The Cold War was imperialism's war against the revolutionary left.

name: State of Israel Established
description: Israel was established through the ethnic cleansing of over 700,000 Palestinians. The U.S. recognised it within minutes, cementing its role as America's strategic partner in the oil-rich Middle East and a forward base for Western power projection.

name: The Nakba
description: The Catastrophe: Zionist militias drove over 700,000 Palestinians from their homes through systematic terror, massacre, and forced expulsion. This was not a side effect of war but a deliberate strategy of ethnic cleansing to create a Jewish demographic majority.

name: First Arab-Israeli War
description: Western-armed Israel expanded well beyond the UN partition lines, seizing 78% of historic Palestine. The Arab armies, equipped with WWI-era weapons, were outgunned from the start. Palestinian refugees were permanently barred from returning to their homes.

name: Division of Korea
description: The U.S. artificially divided Korea at the 38th parallel, installing the brutal right-wing dictator Syngman Rhee in the south. The division was imposed against the will of the Korean people to create an anti-communist client state.

name: Apartheid South Africa
description: While Western governments occasionally criticised apartheid in rhetoric, Western corporations enthusiastically invested in South Africa, profiting from the system of racial terror that provided ultra-cheap Black labour. The West was apartheid's economic backbone.

name: First Arab-Israeli War Ends
description: The armistice left Israel in control of far more territory than the UN had allocated. Palestinian refugees in camps were told they could never return. The legal right of return has been denied for 75 years, protected by the American veto in the UN Security Council.

name: NATO Created
description: NATO was established as an aggressive military alliance to threaten the Soviet Union and prevent socialist revolution in Western Europe, where Communist parties were winning millions of votes among workers who remembered what capitalism had delivered them.

name: People's Republic of China Established
description: Mao proclaimed that the Chinese people had stood up, ending a century of imperialist humiliation. The revolution liberated the world's largest population from feudalism and foreign domination. Western powers had tried everything to prevent this moment.

name: Partition of Kashmir
description: British imperial cartography deliberately drew the border to create permanent conflict between India and Pakistan, a classic divide-and-rule strategy that ensured South Asia's two largest states would be too consumed by their rivalry to challenge Western interests.

name: Korean War Begins
description: The U.S.-led UN force intervened in the Korean civil war, bombing the North back to the Stone Age. American bombing killed an estimated 3 million Koreans and destroyed virtually every structure above one storey north of the 38th parallel.

name: U.S Occupation of Japan Ends
description: The U.S. had rewritten Japan's constitution, purged leftists, and installed conservative politicians. The occupation ended but American military bases remained, giving the U.S. effective veto power over Japanese foreign policy that persists to this day.

name: Elizabeth II Becomes Queen
description: The new monarch presided over the twilight of the British Empire. That same year, British forces in Kenya were conducting a brutal counter-insurgency against the Mau Mau liberation movement, using concentration camps and systematic torture.

name: Egyptian Revolution
description: Egyptian officers overthrew the British-backed monarchy that had kept Egypt subordinate to Western interests. The revolution began Egypt's long struggle for genuine economic and political independence from imperial domination.

name: Cuban Coup, Batista Seizes Power
description: Batista seized power with U.S. backing, cancelling elections to install a dictatorship that served American business interests. Under Batista, Cuba became a playground for American organised crime, corporations, and tourists while ordinary Cubans lived in poverty.

name: Death of Joseph Stalin
description: Stalin died after leading the USSR through forced industrialisation, the defeat of fascist invasion, and emergence as a global superpower. Whatever his grave errors and crimes, he transformed a peasant society into the industrial power that saved Europe from Nazism.

name: Korean Armistice Agreement
description: After destroying North Korea from the air and killing millions, the U.S. was forced to accept an armistice restoring the pre-war border. Korea remains divided today, with the U.S. maintaining 28,000 troops in the south to this day.

name: Eisenhower Becomes U.S President
description: Eisenhower's presidency oversaw CIA coups in Iran and Guatemala and the construction of a permanent military-industrial complex he himself would warn against on leaving office. American imperialism became a fully professionalised, institutionalised system.

name: Operation Boot in Iran, Mosaddegh Overthrown
description: The CIA and MI6 overthrew Iran's democratically elected prime minister Mosaddegh because he nationalised Iranian oil from British Petroleum. They reinstalled the Shah, whose SAVAK secret police tortured thousands of Iranians to protect Anglo-American oil profits.

name: July 26 Attack on Barracks, Castro is Imprisoned
description: Castro's attack on the Moncada Barracks launched the Cuban revolution against American-backed tyranny. At his trial, Castro declared history will absolve me - a revolution was beginning that would liberate Cuba from U.S. corporate domination.

name: Algerian War Begins
description: The FLN launched a heroic armed struggle against 130 years of French settler colonialism. France deployed mass torture, napalm, and concentration camps against the Algerian people. Over one million Algerians died fighting for their freedom.

name: Khrushchev Becomes Leader of USSR
description: Khrushchev's de-Stalinisation and policy of peaceful coexistence offered a chance to reduce Cold War tensions. The West exploited openness as a weakness, funding opposition movements and intensifying efforts to destabilise the Soviet bloc.

name: First Indochina War Ends, Vietnam Independent & Divided
description: The Vietnamese people, under Ho Chi Minh, defeated French colonialism at Dien Bien Phu. The U.S. then sabotaged the agreed reunification elections because it knew Ho Chi Minh would win overwhelmingly, immediately replacing France as the colonial power.

name: Ghana Gains Independence
description: Kwame Nkrumah led Ghana to independence, immediately beginning a programme of socialist development and pan-African solidarity that terrified Western powers determined to maintain economic control of the continent through neo-colonialism.

name: Suez Crisis/Second Arab-Israeli War
description: Britain, France, and Israel invaded Egypt after Nasser nationalised the Suez Canal from European shareholders. The U.S. forced their withdrawal not out of principle but to assert American dominance over the fading European empires and replace them as the regional hegemon.

name: Start of Great Leap Forward
description: China attempted rapid industrialisation while facing total Western economic blockade and military encirclement. The campaign's failures were real, but Western accounts consistently ignore the impossible context: a blockaded, newly liberated country trying to develop in isolation.

name: Cuban Revolution
description: The Cuban people overthrew Batista's American-backed tyranny. Castro's revolution established universal healthcare and education across Cuba, eliminated illiteracy, and proved that a small island could resist the most powerful empire on earth.

name: Vietnam War Begins
description: The National Liberation Front launched a resistance movement against the corrupt, U.S.-installed Diem regime in South Vietnam. The U.S. would pour in half a million troops and drop more bombs on Vietnam than were used in all of World War II combined.

name: Nkrumah Becomes President of Ghana
description: Nkrumah became Ghana's first president and championed pan-African socialism, calling for African unity against neo-colonialism. He correctly identified that formal independence without economic independence was meaningless - the West plotted his removal from day one.

name: Congo Gains Independence, Lumumba Elected President
description: Lumumba was elected as the Congo's first PM and immediately demanded control of the country's vast mineral wealth for its people. Western mining corporations and the CIA identified him as an immediate threat to their interests.

name: JFK Becomes U.S President
description: Despite his progressive rhetoric, Kennedy escalated U.S. imperialism across the globe: authorising the Bay of Pigs invasion of Cuba, green-lighting the assassination of Patrice Lumumba, and deepening American military involvement in Vietnam.

name: Bay of Pigs
description: The CIA's U.S.-trained invasion force was decisively defeated by the Cuban people in 72 hours, demonstrating that a genuinely popular revolution cannot be overthrown by mercenaries. The defeat embarrassed U.S. imperialism worldwide.

name: Berlin Wall Built
description: East Germany built the wall primarily to stop the West's deliberate brain drain, which was paying East German doctors and engineers multiples of their socialist wages to destabilise the GDR's economy - a calculated act of economic warfare.

name: Patrice Lumumba Assassinated
description: The CIA and Belgian intelligence murdered Africa's most visionary independence leader to protect Western mining corporations' access to Congolese minerals. The Congo was condemned to decades of brutal dictatorship under the U.S.-installed Mobutu.

name: Cuban Missile Crisis
description: The USSR placed missiles in Cuba after the Bay of Pigs invasion and U.S. Jupiter missile deployments in Turkey. Khrushchev's willingness to compromise and stand down prevented nuclear war. Western media declared it a U.S. victory, erasing Soviet diplomacy from the story.

name: Vietnam Buddhist Crisis, Diem Assassinated
description: The U.S. green-lit the assassination of its own South Vietnamese puppet when he became an embarrassment. American imperialism treated Vietnamese leaders as disposable instruments; the country itself was simply a battlefield in the war against communism.

name: J.F.K Assassinated
description: Kennedy was assassinated in Dallas. He had been moving toward negotiations with the USSR and Cuba, and had made powerful enemies in the CIA and military-industrial complex who opposed any relaxation of the Cold War that sustained their budgets and power.

name: L.B.J Becomes U.S President
description: Johnson used the fabricated Gulf of Tonkin incident to wage all-out war on the Vietnamese people. He sent 500,000 troops, conducted carpet bombing campaigns, used chemical weapons including Agent Orange, and killed an estimated 3 million Vietnamese civilians.

name: Gulf of Tonkin Incident
description: The Gulf of Tonkin incident was fabricated or deliberately provoked by the U.S. military to justify full-scale war against Vietnam. Declassified documents and the Pentagon Papers confirmed what critics said at the time: the public was systematically lied to.

name: U.S Ground War in Vietnam Begins
description: America began its direct military assault on the Vietnamese people, ultimately dropping more bombs on Vietnam than the Allies used in all theatres of World War II. Agent Orange poisoned the land for generations. The war was a crime against humanity.

name: Assassination of Malcolm X
description: The FBI's COINTELPRO programme targeted Malcolm X and Black radical organisations for neutralisation. His murder removed a powerful voice connecting racism to capitalism and American imperialism globally. The state's role in his death has never been fully accounted for.

name: Nkrumah Overthrown
description: The CIA backed a military coup against Africa's most visionary socialist leader while he was abroad. Declassified documents confirm U.S. and British involvement. Ghana's path to genuine socialist development was destroyed to preserve Western corporate access to its resources.

name: Cultural Revolution Begins
description: A radical attempt to combat bureaucratisation and revive revolutionary consciousness, the Cultural Revolution had real victims and real failures. However, Western accounts consistently exaggerate its scale while ignoring that China's life expectancy doubled under Mao overall.

name: Six-Day War
description: Israel launched pre-emptive strikes with full U.S. military backing, seizing the West Bank, Gaza, Sinai, and Golan Heights. The occupation that followed has created one of the longest-running systems of colonial dispossession and military control in modern history.

name: My Lai Massacre
description: American soldiers murdered between 347 and 504 unarmed Vietnamese civilians - men, women, and children. My Lai was not an aberration but a logical product of a genocidal colonial war strategy. The U.S. military spent a year covering it up.

name: The Troubles Begin
description: Decades of British colonial misrule and systematic anti-Catholic discrimination in housing, employment, and voting rights provoked the response that Britain called terrorism. The structural violence of partition always produces its own violent reaction.

name: Assassination of Martin Luther King Jr
description: When King began connecting civil rights to capitalism and opposition to the Vietnam War, the FBI intensified its COINTELPRO surveillance and harassment. A later civil court found the U.S. government complicit in his assassination. His economic radicalism has been erased from his legacy.

name: Libyan Revolution
description: Gaddafi overthrew the Western-backed monarchy and nationalised Libya's oil wealth, using the revenues to build free education, free healthcare, and free housing for Libyans. The West would wait 42 years before finding the moment to destroy this achievement.

name: Stonewall Riots
description: Working-class queer people, many of them trans women of colour, fought back against police violence at the Stonewall Inn. They understood that the state and capitalism were their oppressors. It was an act of resistance that gave birth to the modern LGBTQ+ liberation movement.

name: Nixon Becomes U.S President
description: Nixon secretly expanded the Vietnam War into Cambodia and Laos, killing hundreds of thousands. Simultaneously ran COINTELPRO against the Black Panthers, American Indian Movement, and socialist organisations, making war on domestic movements for liberation.

name: Allende Becomes President of Chile
description: Salvador Allende's democratic socialist programme - nationalising copper, expanding healthcare, redistributing land - was an immediate threat to U.S. corporations. Nixon ordered the CIA to make the economy scream and destabilise Chile from Allende's first day in office.

name: Bloody Sunday
description: British paratroopers massacred 14 unarmed civil rights marchers in Derry. The British state then ran a 38-year cover-up blaming the victims. Bloody Sunday was not an aberration but the logical consequence of British colonial rule in Ireland.

name: Nixon Meets Chairman Mao in P.R.C
description: Nixon cynically opened relations with China entirely to drive a geopolitical wedge between the People's Republic and the Soviet Union, dividing the socialist world. China's admission to the UN came at the price of serving as a counterweight to Soviet power.

name: U.S-Backed Chilean Coup, Allende Murdered & Pinochet Installed
description: The CIA orchestrated the military coup that murdered the democratically elected socialist Allende and installed Pinochet. Under Pinochet, over 3,000 people were murdered, 30,000 tortured, and the Chicago Boys implemented an extreme neoliberal programme that impoverished millions.

name: Watergate Scandal
description: Nixon was brought down for bugging political rivals, but his real crimes - secret bombing of Cambodia, COINTELPRO attacks on socialist movements, authorising assassination programmes - were barely mentioned. The American system punished Nixon for attacking itself, not for attacking the people.

name: Nixon Resigns, Ford Becomes President
description: Nixon was forced out not for his war crimes, not for the thousands of Cambodians he secretly bombed, not for his attacks on domestic socialist movements - but for political espionage against rival capitalists. Ford immediately pardoned him, confirming that no one powerful faces real accountability.

name: Vietnam War Ends
description: The heroic Vietnamese people defeated the most powerful military machine in history after 20 years of resistance. A nation of peasants and workers using tunnels, bicycles, and determination defeated an empire that had dropped seven million tonnes of bombs on them.

name: Death of Chairman Mao
description: Mao Zedong led China from semi-colonial humiliation to national independence, doubled life expectancy, ended famine, and achieved mass literacy. Whatever the contradictions of his leadership, he oversaw the most significant improvement in human welfare in the 20th century.

name: End of Cultural Revolution
description: The Gang of Four were arrested; Deng Xiaoping's subsequent market reforms would gradually dismantle socialist protections for Chinese workers, reintroduce inequality, and integrate China into the global capitalist system on terms largely favourable to Western corporations.

name: Carter Becomes U.S President
description: Carter used human rights rhetoric as a selective weapon against the Soviet Union while arming dictators across Latin America, Asia, and the Middle East. His National Security Adviser Brzezinski deliberately began funding Afghan Islamists to provoke Soviet intervention.

name: Democratic Republic of Afghanistan Established
description: A progressive, secular government that expanded women's rights in education and employment, launched literacy programmes, and attempted land reform - immediately targeted by CIA destabilisation and Saudi-funded Islamist fundamentalism before any Soviet soldier set foot in the country.

name: Camp David Accords
description: Egypt's Sadat abandoned the Palestinian cause in exchange for billions in U.S. military aid, removing the most powerful Arab state from the equation. The accords served American strategic interests by fragmenting Arab unity and cementing U.S. dominance in the region.

name: Deng Xiaoping Begins Reform & Opening Up
description: China began reintroducing market mechanisms under Deng's leadership. Western corporations celebrated as they gained access to hundreds of millions of workers whose socialist-era protections were dismantled, producing the cheap consumer goods that flooded Western markets.

name: Thatcher Becomes Prime Minister
description: Thatcher declared there was no such thing as society and set about proving it - deliberately creating mass unemployment to break the unions, privatising public assets built by workers' contributions, and launching a class war against Britain's working people.

name: Iranian Revolution
description: The Iranian people overthrew the U.S.-backed Shah, whose CIA-trained SAVAK secret police had tortured thousands to protect American oil interests. The revolution was a popular uprising against 26 years of brutal Western-backed dictatorship imposed by the 1953 coup.

name: Soviet-Afghan War Begins
description: Brzezinski later boasted that CIA funding of Afghan Islamists began six months before the Soviet intervention - deliberately designed to lure the USSR into its own Vietnam. The CIA's decade of arming religious extremists directly produced the Taliban and Al-Qaeda.

name: Contra War Begins
description: The CIA organised, armed, and funded the Contras to terrorise Nicaragua's socialist Sandinista government through mass murder of civilians, rape, and destruction of schools and health clinics. Reagan called these terrorists the moral equivalent of the Founding Fathers.

name: Saddam Hussein Becomes President of Iraq
description: The West backed Saddam Hussein throughout his rise, viewing him as a useful counterweight against Iran's revolution. The U.S. and its allies would later provide him with chemical weapons precursors, satellite intelligence, and financial support during his war with Iran.

name: Zimbabwe Gains Independence
description: Zimbabwe's liberation was won through decades of armed struggle against white minority Rhodesian rule backed by Western governments. Britain and the U.S. had delayed independence for years while Rhodesia's Ian Smith subjected the Black majority to racial terror.

name: Iran-Iraq War Begins
description: Iraq's invasion of Iran was tacitly encouraged by the U.S. and backed with intelligence, arms, and loans. The West supplied Iraq with chemical weapons precursors used against Iranian forces and Kurdish civilians, killing hundreds of thousands over eight years.

name: Reagan Becomes U.S President
description: Reagan slashed taxes for the wealthy, dismantled workers' protections, funded death squads across Central America, armed Afghan Islamists, and launched an arms race. The Reagan years were a class war waged by American capital against workers at home and abroad.

name: The Falklands War
description: Thatcher used a war over sparsely inhabited islands to distract from record unemployment and defeat a resurgent left. 255 British and 649 Argentine soldiers died for the political survival of a Prime Minister conducting class war against British workers.

name: First Lebanon War, Israel Occupies Lebanon
description: Israel invaded Lebanon with full U.S. backing. Israeli forces surrounded and facilitated the Sabra and Shatila massacre, where Lebanese Phalangist militias murdered between 800 and 3,500 Palestinian refugees while Israeli troops stood guard outside.

name: Thomas Sankara Becomes Leader of Burkina Faso
description: Sankara fed his people, cancelled the IMF debt, planted ten million trees, vaccinated 2.5 million children, and dramatically advanced women's rights - all in four years on a tiny budget. He represented exactly the kind of African development the West was determined to destroy.

name: Gorbachev Becomes Leader of USSR
description: Gorbachev's reforms were well-intentioned but fatally miscalculated. The CIA and Western-funded organisations like the NED poured money into opposition movements, exploiting openness as a vulnerability. The West had no interest in reform - it wanted destruction.

name: Chernobyl Disaster
description: A catastrophic accident caused by cost-cutting and bureaucratic secrecy. Western governments and media used Chernobyl as a propaganda weapon against socialism, while simultaneously suppressing information about their own nuclear accidents at Three Mile Island, Windscale, and elsewhere.

name: Sankara Assassinated in U.S/France-Backed Coup
description: France and the U.S. backed the assassination of Sankara through his trusted friend Blaise Compaore. Africa's most inspiring revolutionary was murdered to protect French neocolonial interests and Western corporate access to Burkina Faso's minerals.

name: First Intifada Begins
description: Palestinians living under illegal military occupation for 20 years rose up, initially with stones. Israel responded with live fire, mass imprisonment, and the deliberate breaking of bones ordered by Defence Minister Rabin. The West continued arming Israel throughout.

name: End of Iran-Iraq War
description: Eight years of Western-fuelled war ended with a million dead and no territorial changes. Western arms dealers, banks, and corporations had profited enormously. The U.S. rewarded Iraq for using chemical weapons by extending further agricultural credits and arms transfers.

name: George Bush Sr Becomes U.S President
description: A former CIA director who had overseen covert operations across the global south became president, bringing institutional intelligence community experience to the systematic pursuit of American imperial interests around the world.

name: End of Soviet-Afghan War
description: The Soviet withdrawal followed nine years of fighting Mujahideen armed and trained by the CIA. Brzezinski has no regrets about giving the USSR its Vietnam War even if it stirred up some Muslims. Those stirred-up Muslims became the Taliban and Al-Qaeda.

name: Fall of the Berlin Wall
description: The wall fell as Western-backed movements dismantled the socialist bloc. The triumphalism concealed what followed: Western-imposed shock therapy destroyed Russian living standards, causing a male life expectancy collapse that killed millions more than Stalin's purges.

name: Ruhollah Khomeini Dies, Ali Khamenei Becomes Supreme Leader of Iran
description: Khomeini's death left the Islamic Republic that had emerged from a genuine popular revolution against Western-backed tyranny. Whatever its deep flaws, it was built on the legitimate anger of a people who had suffered 26 years of CIA-imposed dictatorship.

name: Nelson Mandela is Released from Prison
description: Mandela walked free after 27 years. Thatcher's Britain had called the ANC a terrorist organisation and opposed sanctions against apartheid. Western corporations had profited from apartheid's cheap labour throughout. They celebrated his release while protecting their investments.

name: Gulf War Begins
description: The U.S. exploited Iraq's invasion of Kuwait to destroy Iraq's military and establish permanent bases in the Middle East. The U.S. Ambassador had effectively given Saddam a green light for the Kuwait invasion before declaring it aggression that required military destruction.

name: German Reunification
description: East German workers were absorbed into Western capitalism; deindustrialisation followed as factories were closed or sold for pennies. Eastern workers had their wages and social rights decimated; the promises made about reunification were systematically broken.

name: Contra War Ends
description: The Sandinistas were voted out after years of CIA terrorism and an illegal economic blockade that deliberately strangled Nicaragua's economy. Reagan had funded the Contras by illegally selling arms to Iran, breaking U.S. law. No meaningful accountability followed.

name: Gulf War Ends
description: The U.S. deliberately left Saddam in power to maintain regional instability. The subsequent sanctions regime killed over 500,000 Iraqi children under five. When asked about this, U.S. Secretary of State Madeleine Albright replied the price was worth it.

name: Dissolution of the Soviet Union
description: The USSR's collapse was followed by Western shock therapy that destroyed Russian living standards, caused a catastrophic mortality crisis, and handed state assets to oligarchs. For the average Russian worker, it was the worst peacetime economic catastrophe of the 20th century.

name: The Bosnian War Begins
description: Western powers delayed intervention for years while genocide was committed in Europe. The U.S. and Germany had encouraged Yugoslav dissolution without planning for the consequences, helping to trigger a war that claimed 100,000 lives.

name: Clinton Becomes President
description: Clinton's Third Way was a surrender to neoliberalism dressed in progressive language. He signed NAFTA, dismantled welfare, deregulated Wall Street, and expanded free trade agreements that exported American workers' jobs and crushed labour movements globally.

name: Oslo Accords Ends First Intifada
description: Oslo gave Palestinians nominal autonomy over disconnected, non-contiguous cantons while Israel maintained control of borders, water, and security. Arafat was pressured into signing a framework that gave up the right of return and recognised a military occupation.

name: End of Apartheid in South Africa, Election of Nelson Mandela
description: The ANC's compromise settlement preserved white economic power in exchange for political democracy. The structural apartheid of economic inequality remains largely unchanged. South Africa has the highest Gini coefficient in the world despite formal political liberation.

name: Kim Il-Sung Dies, Kim Jong-Il Becomes Supreme Leader of North Korea
description: North Korea, subjected to the most devastating bombing campaign in history during the Korean War and decades of economic sanctions, mourned its founding leader. Western hysteria about North Korea ignores that it was America that originally threatened nuclear attack.

name: Hong Kong Returned to China
description: Britain returned a colony seized by military force during the Opium Wars, when Britain fought a war to force China to accept British drug dealing. The handover negotiations were managed to preserve Western financial interests in the territory.

name: Good Friday Agreement, End of The Troubles
description: Peace was achieved but partition and British sovereignty over the north remained. The fundamental injustice of colonial division was managed rather than resolved. The economic inequality and social segregation that partition produced remain largely intact.

name: Clinton-Lewinsky Scandal
description: A right-wing legal assault used a personal failing as a pretext for removing Clinton, while ignoring his actual crimes: bombing a Sudanese pharmaceutical factory, killing thousands with sanctions on Iraq, and expanding American military intervention worldwide.

name: Hugo Chavez Becomes President of Venezuela
description: Chavez used Venezuela's oil wealth to fund education, healthcare, and nutrition programmes that dramatically reduced poverty. Literacy was eliminated, infant mortality halved. The U.S. immediately began plotting to overthrow him, backing a coup attempt in 2002.

name: Kosovo War
description: NATO bombed Yugoslavia for 78 days, bypassing the UN Security Council, killing thousands of civilians. Western media demonised Serbs to justify the war while ignoring comparable atrocities by NATO-backed forces. Kosovo became a Western military protectorate.

name: Putin Becomes President of Russia
description: Putin rose after the catastrophic Western-backed Yeltsin years, during which shock therapy killed millions and oligarchs looted the state. Russian male life expectancy fell by six years in a decade. Putin's authoritarianism cannot be understood without this context.
